# Go Test Patterns

Test patterns for Go projects using the standard testing package.

## File Naming

- Test files: `{component}_test.go`
- Same package as code being tested
- Or `{package}_test` for black-box testing

## Imports

```go
package componentname

import (
    "testing"

    // For assertions (optional, but recommended)
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/mock"
)
```

## Test Structure

```go
// Tests for ComponentName
//
// Generated by /opensdd:tdd-spec from spec.yaml
// Component: ComponentName

package componentname

import (
    "testing"

    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestFunctionName(t *testing.T) {
    t.Run("describes expected behavior", func(t *testing.T) {
        // Arrange
        component := NewComponentName()
        input := InputType{Field: "value"}

        // Act
        result, err := component.FunctionName(input)

        // Assert
        require.NoError(t, err)
        assert.IsType(t, &OutputType{}, result)
        assert.Equal(t, expected, result.Field)
    })

    t.Run("returns error when condition", func(t *testing.T) {
        // Arrange
        component := NewComponentName()
        input := InputType{Field: "invalid"}

        // Act
        result, err := component.FunctionName(input)

        // Assert
        assert.Nil(t, result)
        assert.Error(t, err)
        assert.IsType(t, &ErrorType{}, err)
    })

    t.Run("handles nil input", func(t *testing.T) {
        // Arrange
        component := NewComponentName()

        // Act
        result, err := component.FunctionName(nil)

        // Assert
        assert.Nil(t, result)
        assert.Error(t, err)
    })
}
```

## Table-Driven Tests

```go
func TestFunctionName(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        input    InputType
        want     *OutputType
        wantErr  bool
        errType  error
    }{
        {
            name:    "valid input returns success",
            input:   InputType{Field: "valid"},
            want:    &OutputType{Success: true},
            wantErr: false,
        },
        {
            name:    "invalid input returns error",
            input:   InputType{Field: "invalid"},
            want:    nil,
            wantErr: true,
            errType: ErrInvalidInput,
        },
        {
            name:    "empty input returns error",
            input:   InputType{Field: ""},
            want:    nil,
            wantErr: true,
            errType: ErrEmptyInput,
        },
    }

    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            component := NewComponentName()

            got, err := component.FunctionName(tt.input)

            if tt.wantErr {
                assert.Error(t, err)
                if tt.errType != nil {
                    assert.ErrorIs(t, err, tt.errType)
                }
                assert.Nil(t, got)
            } else {
                require.NoError(t, err)
                assert.Equal(t, tt.want, got)
            }
        })
    }
}
```

## Assertions (testify)

```go
import (
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// assert: continues on failure
// require: stops test on failure

// Equality
assert.Equal(t, expected, actual)
assert.NotEqual(t, expected, actual)
assert.Nil(t, value)
assert.NotNil(t, value)

// Type checking
assert.IsType(t, &ExpectedType{}, actual)

// Errors
assert.Error(t, err)
assert.NoError(t, err)
assert.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrExpected)
assert.ErrorContains(t, err, "message")

// Boolean
assert.True(t, value)
assert.False(t, value)

// Collections
assert.Contains(t, collection, item)
assert.Len(t, collection, expectedLen)
assert.Empty(t, collection)
assert.NotEmpty(t, collection)

// require versions (stop on failure)
require.NoError(t, err)  // Stop if error
require.NotNil(t, value) // Stop if nil
```

## Assertions (standard library)

```go
func TestWithStandardLib(t *testing.T) {
    got, err := FunctionName(input)

    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
    }

    if got != expected {
        t.Errorf("FunctionName() = %v, want %v", got, expected)
    }
}
```

## Mocking (testify/mock)

```go
import "github.com/stretchr/testify/mock"

// Define mock
type MockDependency struct {
    mock.Mock
}

func (m *MockDependency) MethodName(arg InputType) (OutputType, error) {
    args := m.Called(arg)
    return args.Get(0).(OutputType), args.Error(1)
}

// Use mock in test
func TestWithMock(t *testing.T) {
    mockDep := new(MockDependency)
    mockDep.On("MethodName", mock.Anything).Return(OutputType{Success: true}, nil)

    component := NewComponentName(mockDep)
    result, err := component.FunctionName(input)

    assert.NoError(t, err)
    mockDep.AssertExpectations(t)
    mockDep.AssertCalled(t, "MethodName", expectedArg)
}
```

## Event Testing

```go
func TestEmitsEvent(t *testing.T) {
    // Arrange
    component := NewComponentName()
    eventReceived := make(chan EventType, 1)
    component.OnEvent("EventName", func(e EventType) {
        eventReceived <- e
    })

    // Act
    component.TriggerAction()

    // Assert
    select {
    case event := <-eventReceived:
        assert.Equal(t, expectedValue, event.Field)
    case <-time.After(time.Second):
        t.Fatal("event not received")
    }
}
```

## Benchmark Tests

```go
func BenchmarkFunctionName(b *testing.B) {
    component := NewComponentName()
    input := InputType{Field: "value"}

    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        component.FunctionName(input)
    }
}

// Run: go test -bench=BenchmarkFunctionName -benchmem
```

## Test Helpers

```go
// Helper function for common setup
func setupTest(t *testing.T) (*ComponentName, func()) {
    t.Helper()

    component := NewComponentName()
    cleanup := func() {
        // Cleanup resources
    }

    return component, cleanup
}

func TestWithHelper(t *testing.T) {
    component, cleanup := setupTest(t)
    defer cleanup()

    // Test code
}
```

## Test Data Factories

```go
func createCredentials(opts ...func(*Credentials)) Credentials {
    c := Credentials{
        Email:    "test@example.com",
        Password: "TestPassword123",
    }
    for _, opt := range opts {
        opt(&c)
    }
    return c
}

func withEmail(email string) func(*Credentials) {
    return func(c *Credentials) {
        c.Email = email
    }
}

// Usage
func TestAuthenticatesValidCredentials(t *testing.T) {
    credentials := createCredentials()
    // ...
}

func TestRejectsInvalidEmail(t *testing.T) {
    credentials := createCredentials(withEmail("invalid"))
    // ...
}
```
